workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just came out of account health with Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-14 11:02 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-27 15:29 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery account health

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-P trap (Separation of follow-up draft from pipeline note forces context-switching); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-P failure mode (Separation of follow-up draft from pipeline note forces context-switching)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-P -- Separation of follow-up draft from pipeline note forces context-switching
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
